studyName: Longitudinal Assessment of Methylmercury and Gut Microbes During Pregnancy

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table includes methylmercury levels (in baby's meconium, maternal blood levels at different gestational time points, from maternal stool at different time points, and from maternal hair at different time points), blood hemotocrit values at different time points, and individual maternal habits and data (education, BMI, antibiotic use, flu vaccine information, prior smoking information, prior alcohol use, fish consumption, and amalgams).

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

variables.name: age
variables.description: Maternal age

variables.name: alcohol_previous
variables.description: Did you drink alcohol prior to this pregnancy?

variables.name: amalgams
variables.description: Do you have amalgam fillings?

variables.name: antibiotics
variables.description: Antibiotic treatment between collection of first and second stool samples

variables.name: blood_mehg_early
variables.description: Maternal blood methylmercury, for blood collected during early gestation

variables.name: blood_mehg_late
variables.description: Maternal blood methylmercury, for blood collected during late gestation

variables.description: Maternal blood total mercury, for blood collected during late gestation

variables.name: bmi
variables.description: Maternal first trimester body mass index

variables.description: Maternal education

variables.description: Maternal ethnicity

variables.name: fish_weeklyservings
variables.description: Fish consumption

variables.description: Flu vaccine between collection of first and second stool samples

variables.name: hair_thg_late
variables.description: Maternal hair total mercury, for hair sample corresponding to late gestation

variables.name: hair_thg_pre_early
variables.description: Maternal hair total mercury, for hair sample corresponding to pre-pregnancy and early gestation

variables.name: hematocrit_early
variables.description: Hematocrit during early gestation

variables.name: hematocrit_late
variables.description: Hematocrit during late gestation

variables.name: meconium_mehg

variables.description: Maternal stool methylmercury, for stool collected during early gestation

variables.name: stool_mehg_late
variables.description: Maternal stool methylmercury, for stool collected during late gestation

variables.name: stool_thg_early

variables.description: Maternal stool total mercury, for stool collected during late gestation

tables.description: This sample attributes table includes body site where sample was collected, analyte type, histological type, and gestational period when stool samples were collected.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: GESTATIONAL_PERIOD
variables.description: Gestational period when stool sample was collected

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Tissue Type

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status of the sample

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

tables.description: The subject consent data table contains subject IDs and consent group information.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID
